perturbation.original_text: Reseller means an authorized reseller of Distributor that meets Cisco's then-current requirements for resellers, including, without limitation, the requirements set out in Section 26.0 of this Agreement. In the event Cisco enters into authorization agreements whereby Cisco authorizes particular resellers to purchase Products or Services from Distributor and to resell Products or Services to End User, then "Reseller" shall mean a reseller that is a party to such an authorization agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller means any entity, regardless of authorization status or agreement with Cisco, that purchases Products from Distributor for resale to End Users. In the event Cisco enters into authorization agreements whereby Cisco authorizes particular resellers to purchase Products or Services from Distributor and to resell Products or Services to End User, then "Reseller" shall mean a reseller that is a party to such an authorization agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Reseller.' The original definition requires the reseller to meet Cisco's requirements and potentially be party to an authorization agreement. The modified definition broadens the term to include any entity purchasing for resale, irrespective of Cisco's authorization. This contradicts later sections, specifically Section 3.1, where Distributor is obligated to distribute Product only to Resellers meeting Cisco’s guidelines. By broadening the defintion to an entity that doesn't require authorization with Cisco, it creates ambiguity to whom Distributor is allowed to sell the products too, even when Section 3.1 restrict the sale of products
perturbation.contradicted_text: By this Agreement, Cisco makes, and Distributor accepts, the appointment of Distributor as an authorized, non-exclusive distributor of Products and Services to Resellers located in the Territory. Distributor agrees to use its best efforts to distribute Product solely to Resellers located in the Territory. Those Resellers may resell Product only to End Users who intend to use the Product in the Territory. All Resellers to which Distributor distributes Product must meet Cisco's then-current guidelines for Resellers, as amended from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Reseller' broadens the term to include any entity purchasing for resale, regardless of Cisco's authorization. This directly contradicts Section 3.1, which states that the Distributor must distribute Product solely to Resellers that meet Cisco's guidelines. The original text limited the definition of Reseller to those authorized by Cisco, creating a direct contradiction with the changed definition and the requirement in Section 3.1.

perturbation.original_text: Unauthorized Cisco Product means any genuine Cisco Product or Cisco Service that Distributor purchases or acquires from any party other than Cisco and/or an Approved Source. Unauthorized Cisco Products do not include Non-Genuine Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Unauthorized Cisco Product means any genuine Cisco Product or Cisco Service that Distributor purchases or acquires from any source. Unauthorized Cisco Products include Non-Genuine Products.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Unauthorized Cisco Product.' The original definition specifies that these products are acquired from sources other than Cisco or an Approved Source and explicitly excludes Non-Genuine Products. The modified definition broadens the term to include products from any source and includes Non-Genuine Products. This contradicts Section 3.5, which states that the purchase and resale of Non-Genuine or Unauthorized Cisco Products are not within the scope of the Agreement. By including Non-Genuine Products in the definition of Unauthorized Cisco Products, it creates uncertainty about the scope of the agreement in Section 3.5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor acknowledges that the purchase and Resale of Non-Genuine or Unauthorized Cisco Products are not within the scope of this Agreement and Integrator is not entitled to the rights granted herein with respect to the resale of such Non-Genuine or Unauthorized Cisco Products. For all Unauthorized Cisco Products, Cisco reserves the right to withhold any warranty or Cisco Support on such products, unless such products pass a Cisco equipment inspection and Cisco receives payment for the applicable equipment inspection and/or software license fees, as required in the then​ current Software Transfer and Licensing Policy. A printed copy of the Software Transfe and Licensing Policy, in effect on the Effective Date of this Agreement and available at http://www.cisco.com/warp/public/csc/refurb_equipment/swpolicy.html, shall accompany this agreement. This policy is subject to change without notice. If Cisco determines that Reseller has Resold and/or redistributed Unauthorized Cisco Products purchased from Unauthorized Sources, then Cisco may, at Cisco's sole discretion: (a) audit Reseller's purchase and resale records of Cisco Product and relevant records pursuant to Section 17.0 (Reports and Records) and/or (b) invoice Reseller for all reasonable costs incurred by Cisco in its performance of the Audit and/or (c) suspend all Product shipments to Reseller.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Unauthorized Cisco Product' to include 'Non-Genuine Products.' This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the purchase and resale of 'Non-Genuine or Unauthorized Cisco Products' are outside the scope of the agreement. The modification creates a clear in-text contradiction by including something previously excluded, creating uncertainty about the agreement's scope and enforceability regarding Non-Genuine Products.

perturbation.original_text: All payments shall be made in the currency in which the Price List is quoted for the applicable Purchase Order [*****].
perturbation.changed_text: All payments shall be made in US dollars regardless of the currency in which the Price List is quoted for the applicable Purchase Order [*****].
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original term by mandating all payments in US dollars, regardless of the currency specified in the Price List. This creates a direct conflict as, under the original agreement, Distributor would pay the Purchase Order based on the Price List's listed currency, however, the updated version restrict the payment currency into US dollars. A contradiction is introduced in Section 6.1 where it reffers to the prices for Products being those specified in Cisco's then-current Global or Value Added Distributor Price List, but payment of those products is restricted into being made in US dollars, due to the modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Prices for Products shall be those specified in Cisco's then-current Global or Value Added Distributor Price List. [*****] As provided in sub- section 3.2, in its discretion, Cisco may choose to make products or services which are on the Global Price List but not on the Value Added Distributor Price List available to Distributor. The prices for such products or services will be provided by Cisco to Distributor at the time Cisco makes such products available.
perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates all payments in US dollars, directly contradicting the original agreement where payments were based on the currency specified in the Price List. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text which states that 'Prices for Products shall be those specified in Cisco's then-current Global or Value Added Distributor Price List.' The modification restricts payment currency into US dollars, therefore the original agreement and the contradicted text now conflict.
